full_name: Placement of Aortic Transcatheter Valves — Cohort B
quick_summary: In patients deemed inoperable, balloon-expandable TAVR cut 1-year mortality by 20 absolute points versus standard therapy, creating the prohibitive-risk indication.
takeaway: TAVR transformed prognosis for patients who had no real surgical option — it was never just palliation.
comparator: Standard therapy incl. balloon aortic valvuloplasty
inclusion: ≥2 surgeons agreed surgical risk was prohibitive
key_results: Higher early stroke and vascular complications with TAVR
why_matters: First randomized proof that TAVR beats medical therapy — the trial that made TAVR a legitimate therapy rather than an experimental rescue.
pearls: The comparator was genuinely 'no good option', which magnifies the effect size.
Early-generation device: vascular/stroke rates are far higher than contemporary practice.
limitations: High PVL and vascular complication rates by modern standards
guideline_acc: Underpins Class I TAVR for prohibitive-risk symptomatic severe AS.
guideline_esc: Anchored early ESC support for TAVI when surgery is not feasible.
fda_impact: Basis for first U.S. approval of SAPIEN in inoperable patients (2011).
authors: Leon MB, Smith CR, et al.

full_name: Placement of Aortic Transcatheter Valves — Cohort A
quick_summary: In high-risk operable patients, balloon-expandable TAVR was noninferior to surgery for 1-year survival — the first head-to-head TAVR-vs-SAVR proof.
takeaway: The first randomized demonstration that TAVR can match surgery in high-risk operable AS.
inclusion: High but not prohibitive surgical risk
key_results: More strokes / vascular complications early with TAVR
More major bleeding and new AF with surgery
why_matters: Opened the door to standard-of-care adoption of TAVR in high-risk patients.
pearls: Included transapical access — no longer routine.
Early stroke signal drove years of cerebral-protection research.
guideline_acc: Supports Class I TAVR for high-risk symptomatic severe AS.
guideline_esc: Contributed to ESC recommendation for TAVI in high-risk patients.
fda_impact: Basis for SAPIEN approval in high-risk operable patients (2012).
authors: Smith CR, Leon MB, et al.

full_name: CoreValve U.S. Pivotal Trial — Extreme Risk Iliofemoral Study
quick_summary: A self-expanding valve comfortably beat its pre-specified performance goal in prohibitive-risk patients, establishing a credible alternative platform.
takeaway: The self-expanding platform proved itself in the sickest patients before it was ever tested against surgery.
primary_endpoint: All-cause mortality or major stroke at 12 months vs an OPG of 43%.
key_results: All-cause mortality 24.3%; major stroke 4.3% at 1 year
why_matters: Gave prohibitive-risk patients a second device family and set up the pivotal high-risk randomized trial.
pearls: Single-arm design — benchmarked against a goal, not surgery.
Supra-annular design delivers large effective orifice area.
guideline_acc: Supported extreme-risk indication for self-expanding TAVR.
guideline_esc: Reinforced TAVI where surgery is not feasible.
fda_impact: Basis for CoreValve extreme-risk U.S. approval (2014).
authors: Popma JJ, Adams DH, et al.
journal: Journal of the American College of Cardiology

full_name: CoreValve U.S. Pivotal Trial — High Risk Study
quick_summary: The self-expanding valve was not merely noninferior but SUPERIOR to surgery for 1-year survival — the first TAVR trial to formally beat SAVR.
takeaway: The first RCT to show TAVR superior to surgery for survival — self-expanding TAVR became impossible to ignore.
primary_endpoint: All-cause mortality at 1 year (noninferiority, then superiority tested).
key_results: Lower mortality maintained; large orifice areas
Substantially higher permanent pacemaker rate with TAVR
why_matters: Cemented device-platform diversity and put self-expanding TAVR alongside balloon-expandable in guidelines.
pearls: Note the conduction trade-off: better survival but more pacemakers.
Superiority — not just noninferiority — distinguishes this from PARTNER 1A.
guideline_acc: Supports Class I TAVR (self-expanding) in high-risk severe AS.
guideline_esc: Basis for ESC recognition of self-expanding TAVI in high-risk patients.
fda_impact: Basis for CoreValve high-risk U.S. approval (2014).
authors: Adams DH, Popma JJ, et al.

full_name: Placement of Aortic Transcatheter Valves 2A
quick_summary: SAPIEN XT was noninferior to surgery in intermediate-risk patients, and the transfemoral subgroup did especially well — intermediate-risk TAVR began here.
takeaway: Normalized TAVR for intermediate-risk patients, particularly transfemoral candidates.
primary_endpoint: Death from any cause or disabling stroke at 2 years (noninferiority).
key_results: More vascular complications / PVL with TAVR; more bleeding & AKI with surgery
why_matters: Extended the evidence base below high risk and reframed access route as a key determinant of benefit.
pearls: The transfemoral signal, not the overall result, drove practice change.
Second-generation valve — still pre-SAPIEN 3.
guideline_acc: Central to Class I/IIa intermediate-risk recommendations.
guideline_esc: Key evidence for ESC intermediate-risk guidance.
fda_impact: Supported balloon-expandable expansion to intermediate risk.
authors: Leon MB, Smith CR, et al.

full_name: Surgical Replacement and Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation
quick_summary: The self-expanding counterpart to PARTNER 2A — noninferior to surgery in intermediate-risk patients, confirming platform-agnostic benefit.
takeaway: Self-expanding TAVR performs comparably to surgery in intermediate-risk AS.
primary_endpoint: Death from any cause or disabling stroke at 24 months (noninferiority).
key_results: More pacemakers and moderate PVL with TAVR
Better valve haemodynamics with the supra-annular valve
why_matters: Gave intermediate-risk patients a validated self-expanding option and shaped heart-team decision-making.
pearls: Conduction disturbance remains the main trade-off vs surgery.
Supra-annular design gives lower gradients, useful in smaller annuli.
limitations: Moderate PVL more frequent than SAVR
guideline_esc: Major evidence base for ESC intermediate-risk guidance.
authors: Reardon MJ, Van Mieghem NM, et al.

full_name: United Kingdom Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation Trial
quick_summary: A pragmatic, sponsor-independent UK trial found contemporary TAVI noninferior to surgery in older patients at moderately increased risk.
takeaway: Sponsor-independent data reassured clinicians that TAVI-vs-SAVR results hold up outside industry pivotal trials.
population: Older patients, severe AS, moderately ↑ risk
key_results: Higher pacemaker and PVL with TAVI
why_matters: Demonstrated generalizability beyond tightly controlled, industry-run pivotal trials.
pearls: Operator device choice mirrors real-world practice.
Open-label pragmatic design trades some rigor for external validity.
guideline_acc: Supportive of broader TAVR adoption; not a U.S. registration trial.
fda_impact: No direct U.S. regulatory role (UK/European trial).
authors: Toff WD, et al.

full_name: Placement of Aortic Transcatheter Valves 3
quick_summary: In low-risk transfemoral patients, SAPIEN 3 was superior to surgery at 1 year; by 5–7 years the hard outcomes converge.
takeaway: Moved TAVR from 'acceptable' to a preferred option for many low-risk transfemoral patients — but long-term data show equivalence, not lasting superiority.
primary_endpoint: Composite of death, stroke, or rehospitalization at 1 year.
key_results: Shorter stay, less new AF with TAVR
why_matters: Triggered the low-risk paradigm shift and rapid regulatory expansion.
pearls: Early superiority attenuates over time — critical when counselling younger patients.
Bicuspid and complex anatomies were excluded — don't over-generalize.
guideline_esc: Key evidence behind age/anatomy-based ESC selection.
authors: Mack MJ, Leon MB, et al.

full_name: Evolut Transcatheter Aortic Valve Replacement in Low Risk Patients
quick_summary: Self-expanding TAVR was noninferior to surgery in low-risk patients, with favourable haemodynamics but more pacemakers.
takeaway: Low-risk TAVR is not just a balloon-expandable story — supra-annular valves are durable and haemodynamically attractive, especially in small annuli.
device: Medtronic CoreValve / Evolut R / Evolut PRO
primary_endpoint: Death or disabling stroke at 24 months (noninferiority).
key_results: Lower gradients and less prosthesis–patient mismatch with TAVR
Higher permanent pacemaker rate with TAVR
why_matters: Established durable, haemodynamically favourable low-risk self-expanding TAVR.
pearls: Best haemodynamics in small annuli — foreshadowed the SMART trial.
Conduction disturbance remains the key trade-off.
fda_impact: Directly supported FDA low-risk expansion for self-expanding valves (2019).
authors: Popma JJ, Deeb GM, et al.

full_name: Nordic Aortic Valve Intervention Trial
quick_summary: The first randomized trial in a largely lower-risk, all-comers population — no significant difference through 5 (and 10) years, with less structural valve deterioration but more pacemakers after TAVI.
takeaway: Made low-risk TAVI believable years before formal low-risk approvals.
primary_endpoint: Composite of all-cause death, stroke, or MI at 1 year.
key_results: No significant 1-year difference in the primary composite
Less structural valve deterioration but more pacemakers with TAVI
why_matters: First randomized low-risk signal — conceptual groundwork for PARTNER 3 / Evolut Low Risk.
pearls: Small trial; long follow-up is its real value.
10-year durability data are among the longest available for TAVI.
limitations: Surgical outcomes worse than expected in some analyses
guideline_acc: Conceptually influential; not a registration trial.
guideline_esc: Early support for the low-risk concept.
fda_impact: Not a direct U.S. regulatory trial.
authors: Thyregod HGH, et al.

full_name: Nordic Aortic Valve Intervention 2 — Younger Low-Risk Patients
quick_summary: In younger low-risk patients (≤75), outcomes were less favourable for TAVI overall, driven by excess events in bicuspid anatomy; the tricuspid subgroup was more reassuring.
takeaway: The clearest warning not to over-generalize low-risk TAVR data to younger, bicuspid patients.
primary_endpoint: Composite of death, stroke, or rehospitalization at 1 year.
key_results: Excess events concentrated in bicuspid anatomy
why_matters: Refines what 'low-risk' means once age and bicuspid anatomy enter the picture.
pearls: Bicuspid signal drives the caution — anatomy matters more than risk score in the young.
Underpowered for definitive subgroup conclusions.
guideline_acc: Informs age/anatomy counselling; not practice-resetting alone.
authors: Jørgensen TH, Thyregod HGH, et al.

full_name: Randomized Trial of TAVI vs SAVR in Low-to-Intermediate-Risk Patients
quick_summary: A German, industry-independent RCT: contemporary TAVI (operator's choice) was noninferior to surgery, with lower observed death/stroke, AF and bleeding.
takeaway: The most pragmatic modern RCT showing contemporary TAVI holds up outside narrow, sponsor-run pivotal-trial ecosystems.
primary_endpoint: Composite of all-cause death or stroke at 1 year (noninferiority).
key_results: Primary composite noninferior; all-cause death 2.6% vs 6.2% (HR 0.43)
Less AF and bleeding with TAVI
why_matters: Independent confirmation of low-risk findings without industry funding.
pearls: Operator-choice valves mirror real-world device selection.
Short primary follow-up — durability still to be established.
guideline_acc: Strong contemporary supportive evidence; not a U.S. registration trial.
guideline_esc: Reinforces current heart-team practice in lower-risk patients.
authors: Blankenberg S, Seiffert M, et al.

full_name: Comparison of Transcatheter Heart Valves in High-Risk Patients With Severe AS
quick_summary: The first randomized head-to-head of balloon-expandable vs self-expanding valves: higher acute device success with the balloon-expandable valve.
takeaway: Framed the enduring BE-vs-SE debate around deployment precision vs supra-annular haemodynamics.
key_results: Less residual AR and fewer pacemakers with BE valve
5-year: no significant clinical difference; better forward flow with SE
why_matters: First randomized platform comparison; still cited on device mechanics.
pearls: First-generation devices — mechanics differ from current valves.
Higher SE pacemaker rate reflects the older CoreValve.
guideline_acc: No direct regulatory role; mechanistic reference.
authors: Abdel-Wahab M, et al.

full_name: Repositionable Percutaneous Replacement of Stenotic Aortic Valve III
quick_summary: The fully repositionable Lotus valve met noninferiority with less PVL but roughly double the pacemaker rate; the platform was later withdrawn.
takeaway: You can buy less PVL at the price of more conduction disturbance.
key_results: Safety noninferior (19.0% vs 16.2%); effectiveness superior (15.4% vs 25.5%)
Much less moderate/severe PVL with Lotus
Permanent pacemaker 35.5% vs 19.6% — a major trade-off
why_matters: Proof that valve design changes shift the PVL/pacemaker balance.
pearls: Lotus was withdrawn for commercial/technical reasons — limited current relevance.
Illustrates the PVL-vs-pacemaker design tension cleanly.
limitations: Results less relevant to marketed valves
authors: Reardon MJ, et al.

full_name: Portico Re-sheathable Transcatheter Aortic Valve System U.S. IDE Study
quick_summary: The re-sheathable Portico valve was noninferior to commercial valves for safety and effectiveness, with more early PVL.
takeaway: Validated the re-sheathable self-expanding platform as a viable commercial alternative.
why_matters: Supported development/approval of the Portico → Navitor platform lineage.
pearls: Comparator era predates newest devices.
Re-sheathing aids positioning but early sealing lagged.
authors: Makkar RR, et al.

full_name: ACURATE neo vs SAPIEN 3 in Transfemoral TAVI
quick_summary: ACURATE neo failed to meet noninferiority against SAPIEN 3, largely because of more paravalvular regurgitation.
takeaway: Cooled enthusiasm for ACURATE neo — PVL remained the problem.
primary_endpoint: 30-day composite of safety and efficacy events (VARC) — noninferiority.
key_results: More moderate/severe PVL with ACURATE neo
why_matters: Defined the price of lower radial force / open-cell design: residual PVL.
pearls: Early ACURATE iteration — neo2 later improved sealing.
Focused on early (30-day) events.
fda_impact: Contributed to skepticism about the platform in the U.S.
authors: Lanz J, et al.

full_name: ACURATE neo vs Evolut R/PRO in TAVI
quick_summary: Against Evolut, ACURATE neo again looked clinically inferior for death or stroke, though pacemaker rates were lower.
takeaway: A second negative signal for first-generation ACURATE neo against a self-expanding comparator.
primary_endpoint: All-cause death or any stroke at 1 year (noninferiority).
why_matters: Reinforced that not all self-expanding valves perform equally.
pearls: Lower pacemaker was neo's one advantage.
Open-label; early termination limits precision.
authors: Tamburino C, et al.

full_name: Self-Expandable vs Balloon-Expandable Valves and General vs Local Anesthesia in TAVI
quick_summary: A 2×2 factorial trial found second-generation self-expanding and balloon-expandable valves clinically equivalent, and conscious sedation equivalent to general anaesthesia.
takeaway: Helped cement minimalist transfemoral TAVR and suggested clinical equipoise across flagship platforms.
comparator: Cross-device + conscious sedation vs general anaesthesia
primary_endpoint: Two 30-day composites (valve arm; anaesthesia arm) — equivalence.
key_results: Conscious sedation equivalent to general anaesthesia
1-year: more stroke with SAPIEN 3 (6.9% vs 1.0%) in this small sample
why_matters: Evidence base for valve equipoise and minimalist anaesthesia strategy.
pearls: Underpowered for small clinical differences.
Supports conscious-sedation transfemoral workflows.
authors: Thiele H, et al.

full_name: Small Annuli Randomized to Evolut or SAPIEN Trial
quick_summary: In small annuli, the supra-annular self-expanding valve was clinically noninferior and dramatically reduced bioprosthetic valve dysfunction versus the balloon-expandable valve.
takeaway: In small annuli, supra-annular self-expanding TAVR is the better haemodynamic solution unless conduction/anatomy concerns dominate.
population: Severe AS with small annulus (≤430 mm²)
primary_endpoint: Co-primary: clinical composite (noninferiority) + bioprosthetic valve dysfunction (superiority) at 12 months.
key_results: Bioprosthetic valve dysfunction 9.4% vs 41.6% — superior for self-expanding
Lower gradients and less severe PPM with Evolut
why_matters: Provides direct, randomized device guidance for small annuli — a major, under-studied population (frequently women).
pearls: Practice-defining for small annuli / prosthesis–patient mismatch decisions.
Follow-up still short for durability claims.
guideline_acc: Highly relevant to device selection in small annuli / women.
fda_impact: No indication change; strong device-selection evidence.
authors: Herrmann HC, et al.

full_name: Myval vs Contemporary Transcatheter Heart Valves (LANDMARK)
quick_summary: The Myval balloon-expandable valve was noninferior to contemporary SAPIEN/Evolut valves for early safety and effectiveness.
takeaway: Establishes Myval as a credible contemporary balloon-expandable alternative (chiefly outside the U.S.).
population: Severe symptomatic native AS for TAVI
why_matters: Expands the global competitive landscape of balloon-expandable devices.
pearls: Availability is region-dependent.
Broadens options beyond the two dominant device families.
guideline_acc: Relevant to global device competition; limited U.S. regulatory role.
authors: Baumbach A, et al.

full_name: ACURATE neo2 vs Commercial Valves in All-Risk Severe AS
quick_summary: ACURATE neo2 failed to demonstrate noninferiority versus commercial valves; poor expansion and worse outcomes contributed to the platform's discontinuation.
takeaway: A negative device trial with real market consequences — the ACURATE platform was withdrawn.
population: All-risk severe symptomatic AS indicated for TAVR
primary_endpoint: Composite of death, stroke, or rehospitalization at 1 year (noninferiority).
why_matters: Cautionary tale on device iteration, deployment, and execution vs design.
pearls: Debate over design vs operator execution remains.
Reinforces that neo2 is no longer a contemporary option.
guideline_acc: Platform withdrawn; not a current option.
fda_impact: Contributed to discontinuation of the ACURATE platform (2025).
authors: Makkar RR, et al.

full_name: Repositionable Percutaneous Replacement of Stenotic Aortic Valve I (Feasibility)
quick_summary: First-in-human feasibility of the fully repositionable Lotus valve, with favourable long-term valve function in a tiny cohort.
takeaway: Proof-of-concept for the mechanically expandable, fully repositionable Lotus valve.
key_results: Favourable 5-year valve function in a small cohort
why_matters: Seeded the REPRISE program that produced REPRISE II and III.
pearls: Tiny sample — hypothesis-generating only.
authors: Meredith IT, et al.

full_name: Repositionable Percutaneous Replacement of Stenotic Aortic Valve II
quick_summary: Single-arm pivotal experience with Lotus: met its haemodynamic endpoint with essentially no moderate/severe PVL, but high pacemaker rates.
takeaway: Lotus delivered excellent sealing at the cost of frequent conduction disturbance.
why_matters: Established the sealing advantage that carried into REPRISE III.
pearls: Single-arm; benchmarked against goals not a comparator.
Pacemaker burden foreshadowed REPRISE III.
authors: Meredith IT, et al.
caveat: NCT provisional — not confirmed against a ClinicalTrials.gov record.

full_name: Evaluation of TAVR Compared to Surveillance for Asymptomatic Severe AS
quick_summary: In asymptomatic severe AS, early TAVR was superior to clinical surveillance, driven largely by fewer unplanned cardiovascular hospitalizations.
takeaway: May shift the field from watchful waiting toward earlier intervention in carefully selected asymptomatic severe AS.
primary_endpoint: Composite of death, stroke, or unplanned cardiovascular hospitalization.
key_results: Primary composite favoured early TAVR (26.8% vs surveillance)
Benefit driven largely by fewer unplanned CV hospitalizations
Most surveillance patients ultimately required valve replacement
why_matters: First large RCT to test intervention before symptoms in severe AS.
pearls: Hospitalization-driven composite — parse the components when counselling.
Crossover to treatment in surveillance arm was high.
guideline_acc: Likely to influence upcoming timing recommendations.
authors: Généreux P, et al.

full_name: Early Valve Replacement Guided by Biomarkers of LV Decompensation in Asymptomatic Severe AS
quick_summary: A fibrosis-guided early-intervention strategy did not significantly improve the primary outcome, though unplanned AS hospitalizations fell.
takeaway: Myocardial fibrosis is a compelling risk marker, but EVOLVED did not prove a universal early-intervention strategy.
population: Asymptomatic severe AS with midwall myocardial fibrosis on CMR
inclusion: Midwall late gadolinium enhancement on CMR
primary_endpoint: Composite of all-cause death or unplanned AS-related hospitalization.
key_results: Fewer unplanned AS hospitalizations with early AVR (secondary)
Neutral overall for the enrichment strategy
why_matters: Tests whether imaging enrichment can justify pre-symptomatic intervention — it did not, on its own.
pearls: Contrast with EARLY TAVR: enrichment biomarker vs pure timing.
Event rates lower than anticipated.
guideline_acc: Does not establish routine fibrosis-guided early AVR.
authors: Loganath K, et al.

full_name: Aortic Valve Replacement vs Conservative Treatment in Asymptomatic Severe AS
quick_summary: Early SURGICAL aortic valve replacement improved outcomes versus conservative management in asymptomatic severe AS — important timing context, but not a TAVR trial.
takeaway: Background evidence that earlier intervention helps in asymptomatic severe AS — via surgery, not TAVR.
primary_endpoint: Composite of all-cause death, MI, stroke, or unplanned HF hospitalization.
key_results: Extended follow-up HR ~0.42 favouring early surgery
Supports early intervention in asymptomatic severe AS
why_matters: Alongside RECOVERY and EARLY TAVR, part of the broader shift toward earlier intervention in asymptomatic severe AS.
pearls: This is SURGERY — do not cite as TAVR evidence.
Complements EARLY TAVR's transcatheter timing data.
authors: Banovic M, et al.
caveat: Surgical AVR trial — included for timing context; NOT a TAVR study.

full_name: Transcatheter Aortic Valve Replacement to Unload the Left Ventricle in Patients With Advanced Heart Failure
quick_summary: In moderate AS with heart failure and reduced EF, adding TAVR to optimal medical therapy did not significantly improve the hierarchical outcome.
takeaway: Pre-emptive TAVR for moderate AS in HFrEF is not ready for routine use.
primary_endpoint: Hierarchical composite (win ratio) of death, disabling stroke, HF events, and KCCQ change.
key_results: Win ratio 1.31 (95% CI 0.91–1.88, P=0.14) — not significant
Early quality-of-life signal did not translate into clear clinical benefit
why_matters: Tests whether treating moderate AS early helps failing ventricles — it did not, yet.
pearls: Moderate AS remains a research space, not a routine TAVR indication.
Ongoing PROGRESS/EXPAND-type trials continue to explore moderate AS.
guideline_acc: Does not support routine TAVR for moderate AS.
authors: Van Mieghem NM, et al.
journal: Journal of the American College of Cardiology

full_name: PARTNER II Aortic Valve-in-Valve Registry
quick_summary: Registry data showed favourable survival, haemodynamics, and durable symptom relief for TAVR inside failed surgical bioprostheses.
takeaway: Valve-in-valve TAVR became a mainstream redo strategy for failed surgical bioprostheses.
population: Failed surgical aortic bioprostheses, high risk
key_results: Higher residual gradients in small surgical valves
why_matters: Provided the durable outcome data that made ViV a routine alternative to reoperation.
pearls: Watch residual gradients in small (≤21 mm) surgical valves.
Bioprosthetic valve fracture can help in selected small valves.
guideline_acc: Supports ViV-TAVR for high-risk failed bioprostheses.
guideline_esc: Recognized redo strategy in appropriate patients.
fda_impact: Supported ViV indication for failed surgical bioprostheses.
authors: Webb JG, et al.
journal: Journal of the American College of Cardiology

full_name: Redo-TAVR (TAVR-in-TAVR) International Registry
quick_summary: An international registry showed TAVR-in-TAVR is rare but feasible, with higher procedural success for TAV-in-TAV than TAV-in-SAV in matched analysis.
takeaway: Redo-TAVR is feasible and increasingly relevant to lifetime valve management.
key_results: Higher procedural success for TAV-in-TAV than TAV-in-SAV in matched analysis
Coronary access / obstruction is a key planning concern
why_matters: Central to lifetime-management planning as younger patients receive TAVR first.
pearls: Index valve type dictates future redo and coronary-access feasibility.
Commissural alignment at first TAVR matters for the future.
authors: Landes U, et al.
journal: Journal of the American College of Cardiology

full_name: Valve-in-Valve International Data Registry
quick_summary: The foundational global ViV registry: real-world survival and haemodynamics for TAVR inside failed surgical bioprostheses, with worse outcomes in small or stenotic valves.
takeaway: The dataset that put valve-in-valve TAVR on the map globally.
secondary_endpoints: Residual stenosis vs regurgitation mode of failure
key_results: Worse outcomes with small valves and stenotic (vs regurgitant) failure
Established ViV feasibility across device types
why_matters: First large global evidence base for ViV — shaped device sizing and patient selection.
pearls: Stenotic mode of failure + small valve = highest-risk ViV.
Drove interest in bioprosthetic valve fracture.
fda_impact: Informed ViV device labeling and sizing charts.
authors: Dvir D, et al.

full_name: PARTNER 3 Low-Risk Bicuspid Registry
quick_summary: A registry of carefully selected low-risk bicuspid patients treated with SAPIEN 3 showed 1-year outcomes similar to matched tricuspid PARTNER 3 patients.
takeaway: Selected bicuspid anatomy can do well with SAPIEN 3 — but this is registry, not randomized, evidence.
primary_endpoint: 1-year clinical outcomes vs matched tricuspid cohort.
key_results: Similar 1-year outcomes to matched tricuspid patients
Acceptable PVL and haemodynamics in selected anatomy
why_matters: Supports feasibility of bicuspid TAVR in carefully chosen patients.
pearls: Selection is everything — calcification pattern and asymmetry matter.
Not randomized; excludes the most complex bicuspid morphologies.
guideline_acc: Helpful but not definitive for bicuspid TAVR.
guideline_esc: Bicuspid remains a heart-team, case-by-case decision.
authors: Williams MR, et al.

full_name: Evolut Low-Risk Bicuspid Study
quick_summary: A prospective low-risk bicuspid study with the self-expanding Evolut valve showed favourable 30-day and 3-year outcomes in selected anatomy.
takeaway: Bicuspid TAVR with a self-expanding valve is increasingly feasible — patient selection remains everything.
key_results: Favourable 30-day results in selected bicuspid anatomy
why_matters: Extends confidence in self-expanding bicuspid TAVR in selected patients.
pearls: Supra-annular design manages small/asymmetric anatomy well.
Sizing in bicuspid anatomy is challenging — use annular + supra-annular assessment.
authors: Forrest JK, et al.

full_name: Low-Risk TAVR (LRT) Trial — Bicuspid Arm
quick_summary: An early feasibility registry arm treating low-risk bicuspid patients with favourable 30-day results in carefully selected anatomy.
takeaway: Encouraging early low-risk bicuspid experience — but not enough alone to settle age/anatomy strategy.
key_results: Favourable 30-day results in selected bicuspid patients
Provided early CT/leaflet data in bicuspid anatomy
why_matters: Among the first prospective U.S. low-risk bicuspid TAVR experiences.
pearls: First-mover data — hypothesis-generating.
Complements PARTNER 3 and Evolut bicuspid registries.
authors: Waksman R, et al.

full_name: JenaValve Trilogy in High-Risk Symptomatic Native Aortic Regurgitation
quick_summary: A dedicated transcatheter device met its safety and efficacy performance goals in high-risk patients with native aortic regurgitation — a historically untreatable transcatheter indication.
takeaway: Dedicated devices are finally making native AR a realistic transcatheter indication.
population: High-risk symptomatic moderate-to-severe / severe native AR
inclusion: Symptomatic moderate-to-severe or severe native AR
Suitable anatomy for the dedicated device
primary_endpoint: 30-day safety and 1-year all-cause mortality vs performance goals.
key_results: Met the 30-day safety goal (26.7% event rate)
why_matters: Native AR lacks a leaflet calcium anchor; a dedicated clipping device solves the fixation problem standard TAVR valves cannot.
pearls: Standard balloon/self-expanding valves are unreliable in pure AR — device design matters.
Pacemaker rate is a notable trade-off.
guideline_acc: Potentially field-defining for native AR; regulatory pathway active.
fda_impact: Supports approval pathway for a dedicated native-AR device.
authors: Vahl TP, et al.

full_name: JenaValve Trilogy vs Surgery in Non-High-Risk Aortic Regurgitation
quick_summary: A planned randomized trial comparing the dedicated Trilogy AR device with surgery in non-high-risk aortic regurgitation.
takeaway: Would extend dedicated-AR TAVR evidence into a randomized, lower-risk comparison.
why_matters: A randomized comparison would move dedicated-AR TAVR beyond single-arm evidence.
pearls: One to watch for the future of transcatheter AR therapy.

full_name: J-Valve Transfemoral System Pivotal Trial for Native Aortic Regurgitation
quick_summary: A pivotal trial of the transfemoral J-Valve dedicated device for native aortic regurgitation, begun in late 2024.
takeaway: A second dedicated native-AR device advancing through pivotal testing.
why_matters: Broadens the dedicated-AR device pipeline beyond JenaValve.
pearls: Transfemoral dedicated-AR access is the key differentiator.
Watch for head-to-head positioning vs Trilogy.

full_name: J-Valve Transfemoral Early Feasibility Study (Native AR)
quick_summary: Early feasibility study of the transfemoral J-Valve device for native aortic regurgitation.
takeaway: Early-stage feasibility work supporting the J-Valve AR pivotal program.
why_matters: Feasibility precursor to the JOURNEY pivotal trial.
pearls: Feasibility data shape the pivotal design.

full_name: JenaValve Trilogy vs Surgery — U.S. IDE Randomized Trial (AR)
quick_summary: A U.S. IDE randomized trial comparing the dedicated Trilogy AR device against surgery.
takeaway: The randomized U.S. test that could define transcatheter therapy for native AR.
why_matters: A positive randomized result would establish transcatheter AR therapy in the U.S.
pearls: The AR analogue to the low-risk AS RCTs.

full_name: Hydra Self-Expanding Transcatheter Aortic Valve CE-Mark Study
quick_summary: Early published experience with the Hydra self-expanding valve showed favourable 30-day and 1-year outcomes with large orifice areas.
takeaway: Hydra is a newer-generation regional platform rather than a guideline-moving device.
key_results: Favourable 30-day outcomes; large EOA and low gradients
why_matters: Adds a regional device option; limited penetration in major markets.
pearls: Regionally relevant; sparse comparative data.
Large orifice area is its main selling point.
authors: Reichenspurner H, et al.

full_name: Navitor / Portico NG Global Evaluation Program
quick_summary: An ongoing global evaluation of Abbott's Navitor self-expanding valve, whose differentiator is an active sealing skirt to minimize paravalvular leak.
takeaway: Navitor's pitch is improved sealing / low PVL, with pacemaker still part of the trade-off conversation.
key_results: Low reported moderate-or-worse PVL at 30 days / 1 year in early cohorts
why_matters: Positions Abbott's platform on sealing performance in a competitive market.
pearls: Active sealing skirt targets the PVL problem directly.
Feeds the VANTAGE and ENVISION IDE programs.

full_name: Navitor Intermediate/Low-Risk and Valve-in-Valve Evaluation (OUS)
quick_summary: An outside-U.S. evaluation of the Navitor valve across intermediate/low-risk AS with a valve-in-valve arm.
takeaway: Pushes Navitor into broader-risk contemporary practice conversations (device = Navitor, not DurAVR or J-Valve).
primary_endpoint: Mortality / fatal stroke and PVL performance objectives.
key_results: Very low moderate-or-worse PVL at 30 days in reports
why_matters: Extends Navitor evidence toward lower-risk and ViV use OUS.
pearls: Device is Abbott Navitor — a common point of confusion.
Sealing performance is the headline metric.
fda_impact: Supports OUS expansion; complements ENVISION IDE.

full_name: Navitor U.S. IDE Trial in Low/Intermediate-Risk Severe AS
quick_summary: The U.S. IDE trial evaluating Abbott's Navitor valve against commercial valves in low/intermediate-risk severe AS.
takeaway: The key ongoing trial to watch if Navitor is to compete broadly in lower-risk U.S. markets.
primary_endpoint: Safety and effectiveness vs commercial valves.
why_matters: Potential future U.S. regulatory expansion for the Navitor platform.
pearls: Long horizon — completion projected years out.
Head-to-head vs established valves raises the evidentiary bar.

full_name: DurAVR Biomimetic Single-Piece Valve — EFS and PARADIGM RCT
quick_summary: A biomimetic single-piece transcatheter valve in early feasibility, with a randomized PARADIGM program planned — aiming for surgery-like laminar flow.
takeaway: A frontier device betting on biomimetic haemodynamics and durability.
key_results: Pending — EFS ongoing; PARADIGM RCT planned
why_matters: Tests whether biomimetic single-piece design can improve flow and durability.
pearls: Single-piece biomimetic leaflet is the design bet.
PARADIGM RCT will be the real test.
caveat: Early-stage / ongoing device program (EFS NCT05712161; PARADIGM RCT NCT07194265).

full_name: Cardioband Adjustable Annuloplasty System for Transcatheter Repair of Mitral Regurgitation
quick_summary: A transseptal direct annuloplasty band that cinches the posterior mitral annulus; the multicenter single-arm study showed high procedural success, meaningful annular and MR reduction, and symptomatic improvement in functional MR.
takeaway: Direct transcatheter annuloplasty is feasible and reduces functional MR — but the evidence base is far smaller than TEER.
primary_endpoint: Safety and device performance (procedural success, MR and annular reduction).
key_results: High procedural success with the entire device delivered
MR reduced to ≤2+ in ~90% of early treated patients; ≤mild in a majority at 1 year
Improved heart-failure symptoms and functional capacity
why_matters: First direct transcatheter annuloplasty to reach CE-mark for functional MR, re-creating a surgical-style annuloplasty percutaneously.
pearls: Preserves the leaflets and keeps future options (TEER, replacement) open.
Single-arm data — not a substitute for the randomized TEER evidence base.
limitations: Evidence base far smaller than TEER
guideline_acc: Not a guideline-recommended transcatheter option; TEER is the guideline-supported percutaneous therapy for selected secondary MR.
guideline_esc: CE-mark device; not a class-recommended transcatheter therapy for secondary MR.
fda_impact: CE-mark (EU); investigational in the US (the US pivotal ACTIVE trial was later terminated).
authors: Messika-Zeitoun D, Nickenig G, Latib A, et al.
caveat: Primary registry ID NCT01841554 (NCT01533883 is an alias). Single-arm — no randomized comparator.

full_name: Annular ReduCtion for Transcatheter Treatment of Insufficient Mitral ValvE
quick_summary: The US pivotal randomized trial of the Cardioband direct annuloplasty system plus GDMT versus GDMT alone in functional MR — designed to test both MR reduction and a clinical composite, but terminated before a primary publication.
takeaway: The most important randomized direct-annuloplasty trial — but it was terminated, leaving the randomized evidence gap unfilled.
population: Clinically significant secondary (functional) MR with heart failure
inclusion: Prior HF hospitalization or elevated natriuretic peptides
primary_endpoint: Co-primary: prevalence of MR ≤2+ and a hierarchical composite (CV death, HF hospitalization, 6-minute walk, KCCQ) at 1 year.
key_results: Pending — trial terminated before a primary results publication
why_matters: Would have provided the first randomized clinical-outcome test of direct annuloplasty in functional MR; its termination is why the field still leans on single-arm data.
pearls: Termination reflects the challenge of running device RCTs against a TEER-dominated landscape.
Edwards subsequently emphasized the Cardioband platform for tricuspid disease.
fda_impact: Was the US IDE pivotal for Cardioband mitral; terminated.
caveat: Terminated on ClinicalTrials.gov; no primary results published.

full_name: Mitralign Percutaneous Annuloplasty System (Bident) — First-in-Man / CE-mark Study
quick_summary: A retrograde direct annuloplasty system that plicates the posterior annulus with paired pledgets; the first-in-man / CE-mark study met its 30-day safety and 6-month performance goals with reverse remodeling, but device success was modest and the platform is now historical.
takeaway: Historically important as an early direct-annuloplasty device, but the evidence remained too limited for routine contemporary use.
key_results: Reverse LV remodeling and functional improvement through 6 months
why_matters: One of the first direct transcatheter annuloplasty systems to earn CE-mark, establishing feasibility of retrograde annular plication.
pearls: Small footprint left other options open, but modest device success limited adoption.
The sister Trialign system was explored for tricuspid regurgitation.
guideline_esc: CE-mark obtained (2016); not a class-recommended therapy.
authors: Nickenig G, Schueler R, Dager A, et al.
journal: Journal of the American College of Cardiology
caveat: Device no longer in active clinical development.

full_name: Safety and Efficacy of the Carillon Mitral Contour System in Reducing Functional Mitral Regurgitation
quick_summary: The first blinded, sham-controlled randomized trial of a percutaneous mitral therapy: the coronary-sinus Carillon device significantly reduced mitral regurgitant volume and LV volumes versus sham in HFrEF with functional MR.
takeaway: Carillon reduces MR burden and drives reverse remodeling — a clean proof-of-concept, though outcomes evidence remains limited.
device: Cardiac Dimensions Carillon Mitral Contour System
inclusion: LVEF <50% and LV end-diastolic diameter >55 mm
primary_endpoint: Change in mitral regurgitant volume at 12 months (quantitative echo, intention-to-treat).
key_results: Primary endpoint met: significant reduction in regurgitant volume vs sham
Treatment −7.1 mL/beat vs an increase in the sham arm
Reductions in LV end-diastolic and end-systolic volumes (reverse remodeling)
Trends toward better 6-minute walk, NYHA, and KCCQ
why_matters: First sham-controlled RCT of any percutaneous mitral therapy — validated indirect annuloplasty biology even though it was not powered for clinical outcomes.
pearls: Coronary-sinus approach avoids transseptal access and spares the leaflets.
Proof-of-concept, not a COAPT-style outcomes trial — EMPOWER is the pivotal test.
limitations: Coronary-sinus anatomy and circumflex course limit some patients
guideline_acc: Not guideline-recommended; supportive mechanistic evidence pending EMPOWER outcomes.
guideline_esc: CE-mark device; awaiting outcomes data before any guideline role.
fda_impact: Not FDA-approved; the US/international pivotal (EMPOWER) is ongoing.
authors: Witte KK, Lipiecki J, Siminiak T, et al.

full_name: Assessment of the Carillon Mitral Contour System in Treating Heart Failure With Functional MR
quick_summary: The pivotal, blinded, randomized outcomes trial of the coronary-sinus Carillon device versus control in symptomatic heart failure with at least mild functional MR.
takeaway: The key unresolved trial for Carillon — the first indirect-annuloplasty study powered toward clinical outcomes.
device: Cardiac Dimensions Carillon Mitral Contour System
inclusion: Elevated natriuretic peptides or recent HF hospitalization
primary_endpoint: Safety and efficacy composite (MR reduction and heart-failure clinical outcomes).
why_matters: If positive, EMPOWER would move indirect annuloplasty from proof-of-concept toward an outcomes-based indication.
pearls: Enrolls milder MR (≥1+) than most device trials, reflecting a heart-failure emphasis.
Leaflet-sparing approach preserves future TEER, direct annuloplasty, or replacement options.
fda_impact: Pivotal trial supporting a potential future FDA pathway for Carillon.

full_name: Endovascular Valve Edge-to-Edge Repair Study II
quick_summary: The first randomized TEER-vs-surgery trial: MitraClip was less effective than surgery for the composite efficacy endpoint but markedly safer early, with durable safety over 5 years.
takeaway: TEER is safer upfront than surgery but less durable for reparable MR.
primary_endpoint: Freedom from death, MV surgery/reoperation, or 3+/4+ MR at 12 months.
key_results: Surgery more effective for the composite endpoint at 12 months
MitraClip had markedly lower early major adverse events
5-year data: greater surgical durability but durable TEER safety
why_matters: Established the US pivotal TEER pathway and the high-risk primary-MR niche.
pearls: Residual/recurrent MR is the price of the less-invasive approach in reparable valves.
limitations: Less durable MR reduction than surgery
guideline_acc: Basis for TEER in prohibitive/high-risk primary MR.
guideline_esc: Supported TEER where surgery is high risk.
fda_impact: Foundational for MitraClip US approval (2013, high-risk primary MR).
authors: Feldman T, Foster E, et al.

key_papers.label: 5-year (vs surgery)
key_papers.citation: Feldman T, Kar S, Elmariah S, et al. J Am Coll Cardiol 2015;66(25):2844-2854.

key_papers.label: Renal function substudy
key_papers.citation: Wang A, et al. Circ Cardiovasc Interv 2015;8(1):e001349.

full_name: EVEREST II High Surgical Risk Study / REALISM High-Risk Cohort
quick_summary: A high-/prohibitive-risk MitraClip cohort showing significant MR reduction, symptom improvement, and LV reverse remodeling through 12 months and beyond.
takeaway: TEER became viable for prohibitive-risk MR before the secondary-MR RCTs matured.
population: Severe MR, prohibitive / high surgical risk
primary_endpoint: Acute procedural safety and MR reduction.
key_results: Significant MR reduction and symptom improvement
why_matters: Supported high-risk primary-MR labeling and real-world adoption.
pearls: Single-arm high-risk data preceded the randomized secondary-MR evidence.
fda_impact: Contributed to MitraClip high-risk primary-MR approval.
authors: Glower DD, Kar S, Trento A, et al.
journal: Journal of the American College of Cardiology

key_papers.label: High Risk Study — acute & 12-month
key_papers.citation: Whitlow PL, Feldman T, Pedersen WR, et al. J Am Coll Cardiol 2012;59(2):130-139.

key_papers.label: Functional MR — 1-year
key_papers.citation: Ailawadi G, Lim DS, Mack MJ, et al. Circulation 2019;139(1):37-47.

full_name: Edwards PASCAL CLASP Study (first multicenter PASCAL experience)
quick_summary: The first prospective multicenter experience with the PASCAL TEER system across mixed MR etiologies, showing high survival, durable MR reduction, and functional improvement at 1 year.
takeaway: PASCAL emerged as the main TEER alternative to MitraClip.
key_results: High survival and durable MR reduction through 1 year
why_matters: Established feasibility for the commercial PASCAL program.
pearls: Independent leaflet grasping and a central spacer distinguish PASCAL from MitraClip.
guideline_acc: Feasibility evidence for the PASCAL platform.
fda_impact: Program underpinning later PASCAL pivotal (CLASP IID).
authors: Lim DS, Kar S, Spargias K, et al.

key_papers.label: 1-year
key_papers.citation: Webb JG, Hensey M, Szerlip M, et al. JACC Cardiovasc Interv 2020;13(20):2344-2357.

key_papers.label: 2-year
key_papers.citation: Szerlip M, Spargias KS, Makkar R, et al. JACC Cardiovasc Interv 2021;14(14):1538-1548.

full_name: Edwards PASCAL CLASP IID Pivotal Trial
quick_summary: A randomized head-to-head of PASCAL vs MitraClip in prohibitive-risk degenerative MR; PASCAL was noninferior for safety and effectiveness, underpinning its FDA approval.
takeaway: PASCAL is a credible alternative TEER platform in high-risk degenerative MR.
key_results: PASCAL noninferior to MitraClip for safety and effectiveness
why_matters: Provided the randomized basis for FDA approval of the PASCAL mitral repair system.
pearls: Both devices perform well — platform choice can be anatomy- and operator-driven.
guideline_acc: Supports PASCAL as an alternative TEER device in degenerative MR.
fda_impact: Underpinned FDA approval of the PASCAL system (2022).
authors: Lim DS, Smith RL, Gillam LD, et al.

key_papers.label: 1-year
key_papers.citation: Zahr F, Smith R, Gillam L, et al. JACC Cardiovasc Interv 2023;16(23):2803-2816.

key_papers.label: 2-year (trial & registry)
key_papers.citation: JACC Cardiovasc Interv 2025.

key_papers.label: Anatomically complex DMR
key_papers.citation: Hausleiter J, Lim DS, Gillam LD, et al. J Am Coll Cardiol 2023;81(5):431-442.

key_papers.label: Residual MR & gradient
key_papers.citation: Narang A, et al. JACC Cardiovasc Imaging 2026.

full_name: Percutaneous MitraClip Device or Surgical Mitral Valve Repair in Primary MR
quick_summary: A randomized trial of TEER versus surgical repair in severe primary MR at moderate surgical risk — testing whether TEER can move into earlier-risk degenerative disease.
takeaway: Could determine whether TEER enters earlier-risk primary-MR pathways.
population: Severe primary MR, moderate surgical risk
why_matters: A major ongoing primary-MR trial that could expand TEER indications.
pearls: Watch for durability vs surgery in a lower-risk population.

full_name: Cardiovascular Outcomes Assessment of the MitraClip Percutaneous Therapy for HF Patients With Functional MR
quick_summary: In carefully selected symptomatic HF with severe secondary MR despite maximal GDMT, MitraClip reduced heart-failure hospitalizations and mortality and improved quality of life, with benefit sustained to 5 years.
takeaway: The strongest RCT supporting TEER in carefully selected ventricular secondary MR.
population: Symptomatic HF with 3+/4+ secondary MR despite maximal GDMT
primary_endpoint: Annualized heart-failure hospitalization through 24 months; 12-month safety.
why_matters: Practice-changing; underpinned 2019 FDA expansion to secondary MR and guideline upgrades.
pearls: Selection matters: proportionate MR (high EROA, less-dilated LV) predicts benefit.
Contrast with MITRA-FR explains the 'disproportionate MR' framework.
guideline_acc: Class 2a for TEER in appropriately selected secondary MR (2020 ACC/AHA).
guideline_esc: Supports TEER in selected secondary MR (2021 ESC/EACTS).
fda_impact: Basis for FDA MitraClip expansion to secondary MR (2019).
authors: Stone GW, Lindenfeld J, et al.

key_papers.label: 5-year
key_papers.citation: Stone GW, Abraham WT, Lindenfeld J, et al. N Engl J Med 2023;388(22):2037-2048.

key_papers.label: Predictors of clinical response
key_papers.citation: Grayburn PA, Sannino A, Cohen DJ, et al. J Am Coll Cardiol 2020;76(9).

key_papers.label: Residual MR & quality of life
key_papers.citation: Kar S, Mack MJ, Lindenfeld J, et al. Circulation 2021;144(6):426-437.

key_papers.label: RV–PA coupling
key_papers.citation: Brener MI, et al. JACC Cardiovasc Interv 2021;14(20):2231-2242.

key_papers.label: Sex-specific outcomes
key_papers.citation: Kosmidou I, et al. JACC Heart Fail 2021;9(9):674-683.

key_papers.label: Hospitalizations & mortality
key_papers.citation: Giustino G, et al. J Am Coll Cardiol 2022;80(20):1857-1868.

full_name: Percutaneous Repair with the MitraClip Device for Severe Functional/Secondary MR
quick_summary: In severe secondary MR, adding MitraClip to medical therapy did not reduce death or unplanned heart-failure hospitalization at 1 year — the neutral counterpart to COAPT.
takeaway: TEER is not uniformly beneficial across all secondary-MR phenotypes.
primary_endpoint: Death or unplanned heart-failure hospitalization at 12 months.
key_results: Neutral: no significant reduction in death or HF hospitalization at 1 year
why_matters: Framed the COAPT-vs-MITRA-FR selection debate central to modern SMR practice.
pearls: Enrolled more dilated ventricles / lower EROA than COAPT ('disproportionate' MR).
Explains why patient selection dominates secondary-MR TEER decisions.
limitations: Less stringent GDMT optimization than COAPT
guideline_acc: Defines the selection caveats around secondary-MR TEER.
guideline_esc: Informs restrictive selection in secondary MR.
authors: Obadia J-F, Messika-Zeitoun D, et al.

key_papers.label: 2-year outcomes
key_papers.citation: Iung B, Armoiry X, Vahanian A, et al. Eur J Heart Fail 2019;21(12):1619-1627.

full_name: Randomized Investigation of the MitraClip Device in HF With Clinically Significant Functional MR
quick_summary: The third MitraClip RCT in functional MR: adding TEER to medical therapy lowered cardiovascular death or heart-failure hospitalization and improved symptoms, in a moderate-to-severe FMR population.
takeaway: A modern positive SMR trial that extends TEER benefit toward moderate-to-severe functional MR.
population: HF with moderate-to-severe functional MR, non-surgical
primary_endpoint: Three co-primaries: total HF hospitalizations + CV death at 24 mo; total HF hospitalizations at 24 mo; change in KCCQ at 12 mo.
key_results: Superior to medical therapy across all three primary endpoints
CV death or HF hospitalization reduced (HR ~0.64, P=0.002)
why_matters: Contemporary confirmatory SMR evidence, in a lower-EROA population than COAPT.
pearls: Population sits between MITRA-FR and COAPT on MR severity — read alongside both.
guideline_acc: Strengthens secondary-MR TEER evidence; likely to influence updates.
guideline_esc: Adds contemporary support for TEER in functional MR.
authors: Anker SD, Friede T, von Bardeleben RS, et al.

key_papers.label: Baseline characteristics vs COAPT/MITRA-FR
key_papers.citation: Anker SD, et al. Eur J Heart Fail 2024;26(7):1608-1615.

key_papers.label: Volume-based framework reconciling the 3 RCTs
key_papers.citation: Gupta A, Packer M, Makkar R, Grayburn P. J Am Coll Cardiol 2024;84(24):2376-2379.

full_name: Multicenter Mitral Valve Reconstruction for Advanced Insufficiency of Functional or Ischemic Origin
quick_summary: The first randomized TEER-vs-surgery trial in secondary MR: TEER was noninferior to surgery for the 1-year efficacy composite and had a markedly better 30-day safety profile.
takeaway: In selected secondary MR, TEER now has randomized evidence against surgery, not just GDMT.
population: HF with secondary MR at elevated surgical risk
primary_endpoint: 1-year composite of death, HF hospitalization, MV reintervention, assist-device implantation, or stroke; plus 30-day major-adverse-event safety endpoint.
why_matters: First randomized TEER-vs-surgery trial in secondary MR; likely to influence guidelines.
pearls: Safety advantage driven by less bleeding, reintervention, and new AF.
High-surgical-risk selection — not a low-risk surgery comparison.
guideline_acc: Adds randomized TEER-vs-surgery evidence for secondary MR.
fda_impact: Reinforces TEER positioning vs surgery in secondary MR.
authors: Baldus S, Doenst T, Rudolph V, et al.

full_name: Edwards PASCAL CLASP IIF Functional-MR Cohort
quick_summary: A head-to-head of PASCAL versus MitraClip (each with GDMT) in symptomatic functional MR — the key unresolved PASCAL-vs-MitraClip comparison in secondary MR.
takeaway: The key unresolved PASCAL-vs-MitraClip functional-MR trial.
primary_endpoint: Safety and effectiveness vs MitraClip (see trial pages).
key_results: Pending — mature primary results not identified
why_matters: Would define whether the two TEER platforms differ in functional MR.
pearls: Device-vs-device in FMR is the main open TEER question.
caveat: NCT not confirmed here; part of the CLASP II program.

full_name: Safety and Performance Study of the NeoChord DS1000 Suturing Device
quick_summary: Beating-heart transapical implantation of artificial neochordae for degenerative MR with leaflet prolapse; feasible and safe, supporting CE-mark, though durability vs surgery is unsettled.
takeaway: Beating-heart chordal repair works in selected anatomy, but surgery still sets the durability benchmark.
primary_endpoint: Composite procedural success / MR reduction and major-adverse-event framework.
key_results: Feasible and safe; acute success supported CE-mark
why_matters: CE-mark device; not routine US therapy.
pearls: Best in isolated posterior-leaflet prolapse with favorable anatomy.
timeline: Published: Eur J Cardiothorac Surg / registry
authors: Seeburger J, Rinaldi M, Nielsen SL, et al.
journal: Journal of the American College of Cardiology

key_papers.label: Early European experience
key_papers.citation: Colli A, et al. Eur J Cardiothorac Surg 2018;54(3):460-466.

key_papers.label: Transseptal chordal repair (first-in-human)
key_papers.citation: Rogers JH, Ebner AA, Boyd WD, et al. JACC Cardiovasc Interv 2020;13(11):1383-1385.

full_name: TACT Post-Market Surveillance Registry (NeoChord DS1000)
quick_summary: A post-market registry characterizing real-world CE-mark NeoChord experience and anatomy-based selection.
takeaway: Useful for anatomy selection, not yet definitive vs surgery.
key_results: Registry program active; heterogeneous published outputs
why_matters: Defines real-world CE-mark experience.
pearls: Registry data help refine patient selection.

full_name: Randomized Trial of NeoChord DS1000 vs Open Surgical Repair
quick_summary: A randomized comparison of transapical NeoChord repair against open surgical repair in degenerative mitral disease — the key trial to test durable noninferiority.
takeaway: Potentially pivotal if it can show durable noninferiority in selected leaflet prolapse.
why_matters: The key randomized chordal-repair study.
pearls: Durability vs surgery is the crux.

full_name: Safety and Performance Study of the Harpoon Mitral Valve Repair System
quick_summary: A transventricular beating-heart system that anchors ePTFE neochordae; early feasibility showed favorable 30-day and 1-year MR reduction in selected degenerative MR.
takeaway: A second beating-heart chordal platform with promising anatomy-specific outcomes.
primary_endpoint: Implantation success and MR ≤moderate at 30 days.
key_results: Feasible with favorable 30-day / 1-year MR reduction in selected DMR
why_matters: CE-mark pathway; US investigational.
pearls: Anatomy-specific (posterior prolapse) selection is key.
authors: Gammie JS, et al.
journal: Journal of the American College of Cardiology

full_name: CE-Mark Study for the Harpoon Mitral TRACER Trial
quick_summary: The CE-mark support study for the Harpoon system in severe degenerative MR (mainly posterior leaflet prolapse), evaluating 30-day performance and safety.
takeaway: Important historically, but with limited mature trial-level publication.
key_results: Protocol available; mature indexed primary outcomes incompletely retrieved
why_matters: Important CE-mark program for beating-heart chordal repair.
pearls: Same platform as HARPOON EFS, CE-focused cohort.
limitations: Publication incomplete in the source set
authors: Gammie JS, Bartus K, Gackowski A, et al.
journal: Journal of the American College of Cardiology
caveat: Mature peer-reviewed primary TRACER outcomes incompletely retrieved.

key_papers.label: 1-year outcomes
key_papers.citation: Gammie JS, et al. Eur J Cardiothorac Surg 2021;59(1):199-206.

full_name: Beating-Heart Mitral Valve Repair With the Harpoon System
quick_summary: The current Harpoon program evaluating beating-heart chordal replacement in severe degenerative MR.
takeaway: Ongoing validation of beating-heart chordal replacement.
why_matters: Current Harpoon validation program.
pearls: Watch durability and anatomy selection.

full_name: Global Feasibility Study of the Tendyne Mitral Valve System
quick_summary: The first convincing dedicated-TMVR feasibility experience: transapical Tendyne achieved high implant success with near-complete MR elimination at 30 days.
takeaway: Tendyne was the first dedicated TMVR platform with convincing native-MR feasibility.
population: Severe symptomatic native MR, high surgical risk
why_matters: Foundational dedicated-TMVR feasibility study.
pearls: Repositionable, tetherable design anchored at the LV apex.
fda_impact: Program leading to later FDA approval (2025, MAC indication).
authors: Muller DWM, Farivar RS, et al.
journal: Journal of the American College of Cardiology

key_papers.label: First 100 patients
key_papers.citation: Sorajja P, Moat N, Badhwar V, et al. J Am Coll Cardiol 2019;73(10):1250-1260.

full_name: Expanded Clinical Study of the Tendyne Mitral Valve System
quick_summary: Expanded Tendyne experience showing durable MR elimination and symptom benefit through 2–3 years, though late mortality remained substantial in a high-risk population.
takeaway: TMVR can abolish MR durably, but late mortality reflects the advanced substrate of treated patients.
key_results: Durable MR elimination and symptom benefit through 2–3 years
Substantial late mortality in this high-risk substrate
why_matters: CE-marked in Europe; FDA-approved in 2025 for the MAC-related indication.
pearls: Late mortality tracks comorbidity, not device failure.
fda_impact: Tendyne FDA-approved (2025): first approved TMVR (transapical, MAC-related indication).
authors: Sorajja P, et al.
journal: Journal of the American College of Cardiology

full_name: Clinical Trial to Evaluate the Safety and Effectiveness of the Tendyne TMVR System
quick_summary: The Tendyne US pivotal program, including a randomized TEER-comparison arm and a mitral annular calcification (MAC) cohort; the MAC data supported Tendyne's 2025 FDA approval.
takeaway: The key replacement trial for anatomies unsuitable for repair.
comparator: TEER or surgery strata; plus MAC single-arm cohort
population: Symptomatic MR and TMVR-suitable MAC cohorts
primary_endpoint: Safety and effectiveness of Tendyne in a pivotal framework.
key_results: Randomized TEER-comparison results nuanced; full reporting evolving
why_matters: Highest-profile Tendyne pivotal program.
pearls: Approval landed first for MAC/unsuitable-for-TEER anatomy, not broad MR.
guideline_acc: Basis for the FDA-approved Tendyne indication.
authors: Sorajja P, Thourani VH, Rogers JH
caveat: Randomized (vs TEER) results nuanced; the MAC single-arm cohort supported FDA approval.

full_name: Intrepid Global Pilot Study (Transapical TMVR)
quick_summary: Early transapical Intrepid experience showed feasibility with high MR elimination, informing the later pivotal program.
takeaway: Intrepid validated another transapical dedicated-TMVR route.
why_matters: Foundational Medtronic native-TMVR program.
pearls: Dual-stent self-expanding design conforms to the annulus.
authors: Bapat V, Rajagopal V, Meduri C, et al.
journal: Journal of the American College of Cardiology

full_name: Two-Year Follow-Up of Intrepid Transapical TMVR (Pooled)
quick_summary: Pooled 2-year transapical Intrepid follow-up showed sustained MR elimination and valve performance, though mortality remained meaningful in high-risk patients.
takeaway: Transapical Intrepid works, but patient selection remains the dominant challenge.
key_results: Sustained MR elimination and valve performance
why_matters: Supports the longer-term durability concept for dedicated TMVR.
pearls: Late mortality reflects substrate, not valve dysfunction.
caveat: Pooled pilot/roll-in follow-up; no separate NCT.

full_name: Intrepid Transfemoral-Transseptal TMVR Early Feasibility Study
quick_summary: The transfemoral-transseptal Intrepid EFS showed excellent early safety — no death or stroke at 30 days and near-complete MR elimination — marking the shift away from transapical access.
takeaway: Moving Intrepid from transapical to transseptal materially improved procedural appeal.
key_results: 31/33 implanted; no death or stroke at 30 days
1-year mortality 6.7%; near-complete MR elimination with good gradients
why_matters: Major transseptal-transition milestone for TMVR.
pearls: Transseptal access avoids the morbidity of transapical delivery.
authors: Zahr F, et al.
caveat: NCT not separately identified in the source set.

key_papers.label: 1-year outcomes
key_papers.citation: Zahr F, Song HK, Chadderdon S, et al. JACC Cardiovasc Interv 2023;16(23):2868-2879.

full_name: Transcatheter Mitral Valve Replacement With the Intrepid System (APOLLO)
quick_summary: The flagship Medtronic Intrepid pivotal program, with a randomized surgical-comparison cohort and a single-arm nonsurgical cohort — probably the most important ongoing dedicated-TMVR trial.
takeaway: Probably the most important ongoing dedicated-TMVR pivotal trial.
population: Severe symptomatic MR unsuitable for approved repair or across surgical-risk strata
why_matters: Flagship Medtronic pivotal TMVR program.
pearls: A positive randomized cohort would be a landmark for replacement.
authors: Tang GHL, Rajagopal V, Sorajja P

full_name: Intrepid Transfemoral System in Severe Symptomatic MR (APOLLO-EU)
quick_summary: European expansion of the transfemoral Intrepid program in symptomatic MR or MAC unsuitable for approved repair or surgery.
takeaway: Important for broader transseptal / intended-use anatomies.
population: Symptomatic MR or MAC unsuitable for approved repair/surgery
why_matters: Broadens transseptal Intrepid evidence.
pearls: Complements APOLLO for intended-use anatomies.

full_name: First-in-Human Experience With the SAPIEN M3 Transseptal TMVR System
quick_summary: The first-in-human transseptal SAPIEN M3 experience introduced a fully percutaneous replacement concept using a dock plus a balloon-expandable valve, enabling the ENCIRCLE pivotal.
takeaway: SAPIEN M3 introduced a fully percutaneous replacement concept with a docking system.
population: Severe MR, high-risk / unsuitable for surgery
why_matters: Key Edwards transseptal TMVR proof-of-concept.
pearls: Two-step dock-then-valve approach adapts the SAPIEN platform to the mitral position.
authors: Webb JG, Murdoch DJ, Boone RH, et al.
journal: Journal of the American College of Cardiology
caveat: NCT not confirmed in the source set.

full_name: SAPIEN M3 System Transcatheter Mitral Valve Replacement via Transseptal Access
quick_summary: The first pivotal trial of a fully percutaneous transseptal TMVR system: SAPIEN M3 achieved very low 30-day mortality, near-complete MR elimination, and a 1-year event rate well below the performance goal — leading to CE-mark and FDA approval.
takeaway: ENCIRCLE is the strongest evidence yet that fully percutaneous TMVR is becoming clinically scalable.
population: Symptomatic MR ≥3+ unsuitable for surgery or TEER
primary_endpoint: Non-hierarchical composite of all-cause mortality and HF rehospitalization at 1 year, vs a performance goal of 45%.
key_results: MR ≤1+ in ~96% at 30 days and 1 year; durable symptom/QoL improvement
why_matters: Major contemporary TMVR milestone; supported CE-mark (Apr 2025) and FDA approval (Dec 2025).
pearls: First transseptal TMVR approved in the US — a genuine access-route breakthrough.
Reserved for anatomies unsuitable for TEER; not a TEER replacement.
guideline_acc: Basis for the first approved transseptal TMVR indication.
fda_impact: SAPIEN M3 FDA-approved December 2025 (first transseptal TMVR); CE-mark April 2025.
authors: Guerrero M, Daniels D, et al.

full_name: HighLife Transseptal Mitral Valve Replacement Feasibility Study
quick_summary: A transseptal, docking-ring-based TMVR concept; feasibility showed 90% implant success with 83% device success at 30 days, illustrating both the promise and complexity of multicomponent systems.
takeaway: HighLife demonstrated the feasibility of a transseptal docking-ring TMVR system while highlighting the procedural complexity of multicomponent devices.
population: Symptomatic mitral regurgitation at high surgical risk
key_results: Demonstrated feasibility of a fully transseptal docking-ring TMVR system
why_matters: One of the earliest successful transseptal dedicated TMVR platforms using a subannular docking ring for secure anchoring.
pearls: Two-component (subannular ring + valve) design provides dedicated anchoring independent of annular calcification.
Demonstrated feasibility of fully transseptal TMVR without transapical access.
limitations: Early experience with a technically complex multicomponent system

key_papers.label: 2-year outcomes
key_papers.citation: HighLife Transseptal Mitral Valve Replacement Feasibility Study: 2-Year Outcomes. JACC Cardiovasc Interv. 2026.

full_name: Neovasc Tiara Transapical TMVR Early Feasibility Study
quick_summary: An early feasibility study of the D-shaped transapical Tiara valve, historically important though with limited mature peer-reviewed trial evidence.
takeaway: An important device concept, but mature peer-reviewed trial evidence is limited.
population: Severe symptomatic MR, high surgical risk
primary_endpoint: Freedom from mortality/major adverse events with device performance.
key_results: Protocol available; mature full-cohort primary publication not clearly retrieved
why_matters: Historically important D-shaped transapical TMVR platform.
pearls: D-shaped frame aims to respect mitral anatomy and reduce LVOT interaction.
caveat: Mature full-cohort indexed primary publication not clearly retrieved.

full_name: Cephea Transseptal Mitral Valve System First-in-Human
quick_summary: The first-in-human Cephea implant demonstrated that fully transseptal native-TMVR is technically achievable.
takeaway: Cephea showed that fully transseptal native-TMVR was technically achievable.
population: Symptomatic moderate-severe degenerative or functional MR
why_matters: Important early Abbott TMVR concept study.
pearls: Low-profile disc-shaped design intended for transfemoral-transseptal delivery.
authors: Modine T, Vahl TP, Khalique OK, et al.

full_name: Cephea Transseptal Mitral Valve System Early Feasibility Study
quick_summary: Abbott's early feasibility study of the transseptal Cephea system, with unusually broad inclusion spanning MR, mitral stenosis, and mixed disease.
takeaway: Broader inclusion than pure MR may make Cephea especially interesting.
population: Symptomatic mitral valve disease incl. MR / MS / mixed
primary_endpoint: Safety: freedom from all-cause mortality at 30 days; effectiveness: MR ≤1+ at 30 days.
why_matters: Major Abbott early-feasibility TMVR platform.
pearls: Inclusion of stenosis/mixed disease broadens candidacy.

full_name: AltaValve Atrial-Fixation TMVR Early Feasibility Study
quick_summary: A supra-annular, atrial-fixation TMVR designed to minimize LVOT interaction; early and MAC-focused reports suggest low LVOT obstruction, durable MR reduction, and good gradients.
takeaway: AltaValve may broaden TMVR candidacy by reducing LVOT constraints.
key_results: Durable MR reduction and good gradients in early cohorts
why_matters: Innovative atrial-fixation concept; still investigational.
pearls: Atrial-only fixation is designed to avoid the LVOT and subvalvular apparatus.
timeline: Reports: Structural Heart 2024 / JACC Cardiovasc Interv 2025
journal: Circulation: Cardiovasc Interv / JACC: Cardiovascular Interventions
caveat: EFS NCT not confirmed in the source set.

full_name: AltaValve Pivotal Trial
quick_summary: The pivotal program for the atrial-fixation AltaValve in severe MR.
takeaway: Important next-phase trial for atrial-fixation TMVR.
why_matters: Current pivotal path for AltaValve.
pearls: Atrial fixation could suit MAC and LVOT-constrained anatomy.

full_name: Edwards EVOQUE Eos Mitral Valve Replacement (MISCEND Study)
quick_summary: An active Edwards mitral TMVR program (EVOQUE/CardiAQ lineage) evaluating safety and performance across degenerative and functional MR; mature full-cohort efficacy data remain limited.
takeaway: Important to know it exists, but mature published efficacy data remain limited.
key_results: Active program; mature full-cohort mitral outcomes incompletely extracted
why_matters: Edge Edwards TMVR program (distinct from the tricuspid EVOQUE indication).
pearls: Note: EVOQUE is FDA-approved for tricuspid TR; the mitral program is separate.
caveat: Mature full-cohort mitral outcomes incompletely retrieved.

full_name: Mitral Implantation of Transcatheter Valves
quick_summary: The foundational prospective dataset for transcatheter mitral re-replacement across three use-cases — failed surgical valves, rings, and severe mitral annular calcification.
takeaway: ViV is mature; ViR is feasible but harder; ViMAC remains highest-risk.
population: Failing surgical mitral prostheses, rings, or severe MAC
primary_endpoint: Feasibility / safety across ViV, ViR, and ViMAC.
key_results: Feasibility established across all three use-cases
ViMAC carried the highest mortality and technical risk
why_matters: Foundational transcatheter mitral re-replacement dataset.
pearls: Risk gradient: ViV < ViR < ViMAC.
ViMAC LVOT obstruction risk drives LAMPOON and dedicated ViMAC trials.
guideline_acc: Supports ViV as a redo strategy.
authors: Guerrero M, et al.

key_papers.label: Valve-in-valve arm — 1-year
key_papers.citation: Guerrero M, Pursnani A, Narang A, et al. JACC Cardiovasc Interv 2021;14(8):859-872.

full_name: PARTNER 3 Trial — Mitral Valve-in-Valve
quick_summary: A prospective transseptal SAPIEN 3 mitral valve-in-valve cohort in failing surgical bioprostheses recorded no death or stroke through 1 year, with good hemodynamics.
takeaway: Transseptal MViV with SAPIEN 3 is now a highly credible alternative in failing bioprostheses.
population: Failing surgical mitral bioprostheses, intermediate surgical risk
primary_endpoint: Composite of all-cause mortality and stroke at 1 year.
key_results: No primary endpoint events through 1 year
why_matters: Best contemporary prospective MViV dataset.
pearls: Transseptal access is now standard for mitral ViV.
Watch residual gradients in small surgical valves.
guideline_acc: Supports MViV for failed surgical bioprostheses.

full_name: One-Year Outcomes of Mitral Valve-in-Valve Using the SAPIEN 3 Valve (STS/ACC-TVT)
quick_summary: Real-world registry outcomes for SAPIEN 3 mitral valve-in-valve show high technical success and low 30-day and 1-year mortality.
takeaway: Registry data strongly support MViV as the leading TMVR niche.
why_matters: Reinforced real-world MViV adoption.
pearls: Registry breadth complements the smaller prospective PARTNER 3 MViV cohort.
authors: Whisenant B, Kapadia SR, Eleid MF, et al.

key_papers.label: Contemporary transseptal MViV outcomes
key_papers.citation: Circulation 2024;150.

key_papers.label: 3-year outcomes
key_papers.citation: JACC Cardiovasc Interv 2025.

full_name: MITRAL II Pivotal Trial (Valve-in-MAC)
quick_summary: A dedicated pivotal attempt to formalize evidence for transcatheter mitral replacement in severe mitral annular calcification — the highest-risk re-replacement indication.
takeaway: ViMAC remains the 'last frontier' re-replacement indication.
population: Severe symptomatic MAC, prohibitive surgical risk
why_matters: Dedicated attempt to formalize ViMAC evidence.
pearls: LVOT obstruction and paravalvular leak are the key ViMAC challenges.

full_name: Intentional Laceration of the Anterior Mitral Leaflet to Prevent LVOT Obstruction During TMVR
quick_summary: A TMVR-enabling technique that lacerates the anterior mitral leaflet to prevent LVOT obstruction; the IDE study achieved 100% procedure survival with acceptable LVOT gradients.
takeaway: LAMPOON expanded TMVR eligibility for anatomies otherwise excluded by LVOT risk.
population: Valve-in-ring and native MAC at prohibitive LVOT-obstruction risk
primary_endpoint: Procedure survival with successful LAMPOON/TMVR and acceptable LVOT gradient.
why_matters: Essential TMVR-enabling adjunctive study.
pearls: Makes otherwise-excluded ViR/ViMAC anatomies treatable.
A procedural technique, not a device — pairs with balloon-expandable TMVR.
fda_impact: Established the LAMPOON technique for LVOT protection.
authors: Khan JM, et al.
journal: Journal of the American College of Cardiology
